AI Tech Lead Framework · Functional walkthrough
Team enablement · concrete operating guide

What actually happens
when you ask the AI to code?

One feature, followed from installation to merge. Every slide answers: what triggers, which file runs, what goes in, what comes out, what you see, and what you still own.

Developer: “Add CSV export to the dashboard. Only admins can export.” We will follow this request through the real framework.

Install

How the framework enters a repository.

Understand

How repository truth reaches the assistant.

Control

How writes are routed, blocked, and checked.

Prove

How the team decides the change is acceptable.

This is not a component catalogue. Keep returning to the CSV export example. The developer remains accountable at every human decision point.

01Before anyone prompts

Installation puts an operating system inside the repo

PS> ..\ai-tech-lead\install.ps1 -Target C:\src\Dashboard Detected: dotnet Selected: dist/dotnet Archived conflicting originals Installed framework files
Detect.sln/.csproj, Angular files, or both
→
Selectdotnet · angular · monorepo
→
Archivepreserve collisions
→
Copychosen dist into target
Deterministic

Installer scripts

install.ps1/install.sh delegate to the selected distribution's installer. Greenfield and brownfield modes are tested.

Human

Your responsibility

Review archived collisions, commit the installed framework, and do not assume generic placeholders know your architecture.

Next action

Bootstrap the repository

Run /bootstrap so project name, structure, commands, conventions, packages, and hazards reflect this codebase.

Failure path

No silent overwrite

If the installer cannot preserve or reconcile existing material, adoption must stop for review rather than deleting it.

The framework is committed repository content, not a globally installed service. Mixed .NET and Angular targets select the monorepo distribution.

02What was installed

The files have different jobs and different loading times

ArtifactWhen it participatesWhat it doesWho maintains it
CLAUDE.mdEvery Claude sessionCanonical verification, leanness, SOLID, conventions, structure, routing, Boy Scout rulesTech lead + team
AGENTS.mdCompatible non-Claude agentsGenerated portable mirror of the canonical rulesGenerated—do not hand-edit
.claude/commands/*.mdExplicit slash command or routed intentDetailed Feature/Fix/Refactor/Test/Review/Design/Debt proceduresFramework; customise deliberately
.claude/hooks/*Session, prompt, write, post-write, stop eventsInject context, route intent, block defined writes, run feedback, record auditFramework/tooling owner
docs/*Planning, execution, reviewArchitecture, defaults, playbook, ADRs, review guidance, enforcement truthTeam
TECH_DEBT.md / SECURITY_FINDINGS.md / LEARNINGS.mdSession context and relevant workflowsPreserve unfinished risks and recurring knowledgeTeam
scripts/* + tests/*Local verification and CIMachine-checkable build, docs, architecture, impact, and framework controlsTeam + platform owner
Key distinction: reading a Markdown rule guides a model; executing a hook or CI script performs a machine action. The repository contains both.

Explain that generated mirrors exist for tool portability. The canonical project truth must not fragment across separately edited assistant files.

03Opening the workspace

A session starts by loading stable rules and bounded live state

1 · Assistant-native load

CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md

Stable repository rules: verification, architecture, conventions, workflows, leanness, SOLID, and Boy Scout expectations.

2 · SessionStart registration
.claude/settings.json SessionStart → .claude/hooks/session-start.ps1
Representative injected context
Branch: feature/dashboard-export Recent commits: 3 summaries Adoption state: bootstrap complete Relevant debt: TD-014 Dashboard query duplication Overdue security: none Wiki: team index summary Hazard: ReportingService — architecture entry stale
Context

Why it is separate

Volatile Git and risk state stays out of stable rule files, preserving relevance and prompt-cache stability.

Developer sees

A prepared assistant

You should not need to re-explain the branch, recent work, registered debt, or known hazards every time.

Degradation

If hooks are unavailable

Stable rules still load, but live context may not. Run scripts/framework-doctor.* to see the real machine state.

SessionStart is deliberately fast and capped. It is not a repository-wide scan or a replacement for the assistant inspecting relevant code.

04The developer asks

The prompt is classified before the assistant responds

Event delivered to the hook
UserPromptSubmit { "prompt": "Add CSV export to the dashboard. Only admins can export.", "hook_event_name": "UserPromptSubmit" }
File that runs

.claude/hooks/route-prompt.ps1

The Bash twin performs the same role. Explicit slash commands bypass classification because the developer already chose a workflow.

Classifier result
Primary intent: feature Security overlay: yes (admin/auth surface) Inject: Feature rails Inject: Security-sensitive checklist Inject: Plan gate
Priority: review → debt → design → test → fix → refactor → feature. A question with no implementation imperative takes the answer-only carve-out.
Instruction + salience

Not an autonomous dispatcher

The hook adds the appropriate methodology to model context; it does not secretly run application code.

Developer option

Remove ambiguity

Use /feature add CSV export... when you want the explicit contract without relying on classification.

Surface caveat

Client support varies

Claude consumes this natively. Copilot injection depends on client version and VS Code Preview hooks.

Security is an overlay because the request is still a feature. It adds auth/authorization/threat considerations; it does not replace the Feature workflow.

05What the assistant now receives

The working context is layered—not one giant magic prompt

Tool/system contractassistant capabilities and environment
+
Repository rulesCLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md
+
Session contextGit · debt · security · hazards
+
User requestCSV export + admin restriction
+
Routed railsFeature + Security + Plan gate

Then it inspects

Relevant source, tests, architecture, defaults, existing export patterns, and registered decisions.

It does not know

Unwritten team knowledge, unstated business rules, production facts, or whether stale documentation is trustworthy.

You provide

Acceptance criteria, constraints, examples, rollout needs, and answers to material open questions.

It must disclose

Assumptions, unresolved choices, and checks it could not run.

Functional implication: better repository truth produces better plans. The framework reduces repeated prompting; it cannot recover knowledge the team never recorded.

The assistant may retrieve additional files during reasoning. This diagram shows the stable and event-injected foundations, not every byte of a vendor-specific prompt.

06The plan gate

No code until the team can inspect the intended change

What the assistant should present
Proposed change 1. Add ExportDashboardCsv query/service method 2. Reuse existing dashboard filters 3. Add admin authorization policy at endpoint 4. Stream CSV response; no temporary server file 5. Add service + endpoint authorization tests Files: DashboardController.cs, ReportingService.cs, ReportingServiceTests.cs, DashboardAuthTests.cs Risk: formula injection; large result memory use Verify: targeted tests → solution build → relevant suite
Developer

Approve the intent

Check scope, architecture, permissions, failure modes, and test strategy. Correct wrong assumptions now.

Assistant

Wait on material choices

Clarify questions that change the result; do not bury unresolved product decisions in implementation.

Debt radar

Inspect nearby registered debt

debt-radar may surface TD-014. Bundle it only if it shares the blast radius and marginal effort is small.

Stop condition

Design first when needed

A non-trivial feature without an agreed approach should use /design and produce a reviewed spec before code.

The plan gate is a human comprehension checkpoint. It is not a bureaucratic approval for trivial answer-only work.

07The Feature contract

The agent executes a prescribed sequence

1

Design check

Read an existing spec; otherwise inspect patterns, layers, failure modes, tests, leanness, and nearby debt.

2

Ordered subtasks

Complete one coherent slice at a time. For the example: service/query → endpoint/auth → integration behaviour.

3

Verify each slice

Run the stack-specific build and relevant tests after the coherent subtask—not merely once at the end.

4

Boy Scout touched files

Make bounded, low-risk improvements. Record larger work instead of expanding the feature invisibly.

5

Self-review

Check conventions, build, tests, lint, architecture, debt resolution, and documentation drift.

6

Present evidence

Summarise implementation, named commands run, observed results, unverified assertions, and open drift.

.claude/commands/feature.md contains the Feature-specific procedure; .claude/workflow.md supplies the shared self-review and drift tail.

This is where the methodology becomes repeatable. Fix, Refactor, Test, Review, Design, and Debt substitute different evidence and mutation rules.

08Immediately before an editor write

The write guard inspects the proposed content

Representative PreToolUse payload
{ "tool_name": "Edit", "tool_input": { "file_path": "DashboardController.cs", "new_string": "[Authorize(Policy=\"Admin\")] ..." } }
Registration
.claude/settings.json PreToolUse matcher: Write|Edit → .claude/hooks/guard.ps1
Guard decisions
Allow

Clean content

Exit 0 with no blocking output. The editor write proceeds.

Deny

Defined pattern

Hardcoded secret, warning/type suppression, focused/skipped test, or tautological assertion produces a surface-specific block.

BLOCKED: proposed write contains a warning suppression. Fix the underlying warning; do not silence it.
Scope boundary: the guard sees editor/file-write tool payloads. A shell command such as echo ... > file does not carry that payload, so the hard block does not fire. Rules, CI, and review still bind that path.

Be precise: this is a finite high-confidence pattern floor, not semantic proof that every unsafe write is impossible.

09Immediately after a successful write

Fast feedback and traceability run automatically

Write succeedsDashboardController.cs changed
→
PostToolUsesettings matcher fires
→
post-writefast build/type feedback
+
audit-trailappend repo-relative path
What the assistant may receive
dotnet build failed DashboardController.cs(84,18): CS1061 'IReportingService' has no ExportCsvAsync Framework response: surface the failure to the model so it can correct the incomplete interface/service change.
What is recorded
.claude/ai-audit.log 2026-07-20T11:02:10Z feature/dashboard-export src/Web/DashboardController.cs
Audit records that an AI write occurred. It does not prove the change is correct, approved, or wholly AI-generated.

Fast, not exhaustive

Per-write feedback favours compilation/type checking. Full tests remain explicit workflow steps because they are too expensive after every file.

Client-dependent visibility

The hook process may run even when a client version discards its added context. Framework doctor and canaries expose this.

Failure is not hidden

A build failure should drive correction. The framework forbids “making green” by suppressing the signal.

Post-write behavior is stack-specific. .NET uses solution build feedback; Angular uses its configured type/build checks.

10The verified subtask loop

Implementation advances only with observed evidence

SubtaskFiles changedCommand runEvidence required before continuing
1 · Query and CSV serviceInterface, service, unit testsdotnet test ... --filter ReportingServiceCSV headers, escaping, filters, cancellation, formula-injection behaviour pass
2 · Endpoint and admin policyController, policy wiring, auth testsdotnet test ... --filter DashboardExportAdmin receives 200/export; non-admin receives forbidden; unauthenticated rejected
3 · Integrated featureAll touched filesdotnet build Dashboard.slnCompilation/analyzers clean; no warnings hidden
4 · Relevant regression scopeNo new mutationConfigured relevant suiteExisting dashboard/reporting behaviour remains green
Developer

Challenge test meaning

Would the test fail if authorization were removed or CSV escaping broke? Passing tautologies are not evidence.

Assistant

Name exact results

Report which commands ran, exit status, test counts where available, and anything not run.

Never

Do not defeat the suite

No skipped/focused tests, weakened assertions, ignored analyzers, swallowed exceptions, or tests rewritten merely to accept a bug.

The commands shown are illustrative for the running example. The bootstrapped repository's Common Tasks and stack commands are authoritative.

11Before the agent says “done”

Three different review mechanisms run

Self-review

Agent re-reads its own diff

Checks conventions, scope, build/tests/lint, architecture, resolved debt, and documentation drift. Required by .claude/workflow.md.

Focused agents

Independent lenses

solid-check, convention-check, and debt-radar inspect bounded concerns and return findings; they do not merge code.

Stop hook

Boy Scout nudge

boy-scout-check scans the uncommitted diff, reports cleanup candidates, and deduplicates unchanged finding sets per machine.

Implementation: admin-only CSV export added Verified: ReportingService tests PASS (18) Verified: DashboardExport auth tests PASS (6) Verified: dotnet build PASS Not run: full solution integration suite Drift: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md should mention export path Debt: TD-014 partially reduced; entry retained

Silence ≠ resolution

The Boy Scout hash suppresses an unchanged repeated finding set. It may mean “already reported,” not “fixed.”

Model review ≠ human review

All advisory mechanisms can miss semantic defects or share the same wrong assumption.

Specialist agents are useful for coverage and separation of concerns, not an authority transfer. The final response is a handoff for human inspection.

12The human review

The reviewer checks the change and the developer's understanding

Use /review for a structured read-only pass

Correctness

Does export match business rules, filtering, authorization, scale, and failure behaviour?

Test sensitivity

Do tests prove admin-only access and meaningful CSV semantics?

Architecture

Did the endpoint delegate appropriately? Are dependencies and responsibilities consistent?

Security

Authorization placement, formula injection, information exposure, logging, and resource exhaustion.

Ask the developer
Why is authorization enforced here? What alternative did you reject? How does CSV escaping work? Which test fails if the admin policy disappears? What happens for 500,000 rows? What did the assistant assume that you verified?
Ownership test: if the developer cannot explain the code, its risks, and its evidence, the change is not ready regardless of who or what wrote it.

The framework can inspect code; the reviewer also evaluates whether the developer understands and can operate the result.

13CI and merge

CI repeats deterministic acceptance independently

Push / PRGit carries the proposed diff
→
Build + testscompiler · analyzers · suites
→
Architecturedependency and boundary rules
→
Framework checksdocs · wiki · instruction drift
→
Human mergerisk and fitness decision
Gate

Build/test

Re-runs outside the assistant's session. Local success is not trusted as the only evidence.

Gate

Architecture/static

.NET architecture tests or Angular dependency/ESLint rules enforce configured machine-checkable boundaries.

Gate

Documentation

docs-sync-check, wiki checks, and template checks identify stale or inconsistent operational material.

Authority

Reviewer/owner

Interprets evidence, accepts residual risk, handles exceptions, and controls merge/release.

Host-agnostic: examples support GitHub CI and Bamboo/Bitbucket-style environments. The framework does not require a GitHub-hosted repository.

Which jobs are mandatory depends on the consuming team's CI wiring. Installation supplies scripts and examples; platform owners must connect the intended gates to protected merge paths.

14What each person does

The framework is shared process, not an AI autopilot

RoleEveryday responsibilityWhen to stop or escalate
DeveloperState acceptance criteria; choose or confirm workflow; review plan; understand diffs; run/inspect evidence; maintain relevant records.Material ambiguity, unfamiliar architecture, unverified security assumption, failing checks, or code you cannot explain.
ReviewerReview business correctness, security, architecture, tests, scope, and developer understanding; use /review as an additional lens.Evidence does not match claims, tests lack defect sensitivity, residual risk lacks an owner, or generated code is not understood.
Tech leadOwn CLAUDE.md conventions, architecture/defaults, workflow exceptions, debt/security priorities, and ADR quality.Rules conflict, repeated model failure reveals missing guidance, or a proposed convention changes team-wide behaviour.
Platform/toolingInstall/update distributions, wire CI, maintain client versions and hook prerequisites, run framework doctor, verify cross-shell behaviour.Hooks are unavailable, CI gates are advisory only, client updates change payload semantics, or diagnostics disagree with documentation.
Product/securityProvide business constraints, threat expectations, regulatory requirements, and explicit acceptance of relevant residual risk.The assistant or delivery team is being asked to invent policy, permission semantics, retention, or compliance requirements.
Junior developers get more structure, not less accountability. The plan and evidence make coaching easier; they do not make unreviewed generated output safe.

This slide is the team operating contract. Tailor role names to the organisation, but do not remove ownership.

15Exceptions and failures

What happens when the happy path breaks

Guard blocks a write

Fix the underlying secret, suppression, or test-defeat. Do not switch to shell writing merely to bypass the guard.

Build/test fails

Investigate the signal. Reproduce, correct the root cause, and rerun. Never weaken the check to manufacture green.

Hook is unavailable

Framework doctor reports the gap. Rules become the floor; run the equivalent script manually and rely on CI until tooling is restored.

Plan changes materially

Stop, explain new evidence and trade-offs, and re-confirm scope rather than silently diverging.

Hotfix or incident

Boy Scout cleanup may be skipped with a documented reason/TODO and later /debt follow-up. Secrets and suppression rules remain binding.

Framework advice is wrong

Do not obey stale documentation blindly. Verify repository reality, correct the change, then update the canonical rule or record the decision.

No invisible escape hatch: exceptions need an explicit human owner, reason, bounded duration where applicable, and follow-up evidence.

Friction is intentional at high-risk points. The framework should make degradation visible rather than pretending every control is active.

16Changing the team's standards

When the team learns, update the source—not every prompt

Observerepeated correction or new decision
→
Decideteam agreement / ADR where material
→
Edit canonical truthCLAUDE.md · docs · defaults
→
Regenerate/generate-copilot mirrors
→
Validate/docs-sync + CI
Example learning: “CSV exports must neutralise spreadsheet formulas.” 1. Record architecture/security decision 2. Add project convention and reusable implementation pattern 3. Add deterministic test/static backstop where practical 4. Regenerate AGENTS.md/Copilot material 5. Remove or update resolved SECURITY_FINDINGS entry

Rule

Stable, broadly applicable team behaviour belongs in canonical instructions.

Decision

Material architectural rationale belongs in an ADR.

Learning

A recurring correction worth preserving belongs in LEARNINGS.md.

Unfinished risk

Deferred remediation remains visible in debt or security registers with ownership.

The objective is organisational learning: one correction should improve future sessions for every team member and tool.

17Where the installed framework comes from

The product itself has a deterministic supply chain

src/coreshared shipped artifacts
+
src/stacks/*stack snippets and overrides
→
scripts/build.*resolve stack markers
→
dist/*generated installable products
→
Release/installversioned delivery

Author once

Shared behaviour changes in source. Generated distributions are never hand-edited.

Prove freshness

A rebuild must reproduce committed dist output exactly.

Protect parity

Rule mirrors and PowerShell/Bash twins are validated across the three distributions.

Protect the boundary

no-meta-leak and no-dead-instruction prevent maintainer leakage and fictional commands.

This supply chain is for framework maintainers. Product teams normally interact with the installed distribution, not src or composer internals.

Only dist contents reach consumers. The authoring backlog, maintainer decisions, and meta-development tooling are excluded from installation.

18The complete running example

One request, all participating mechanisms

Open workspacerules load + SessionStart context
→
Ask for exportUserPromptSubmit
→
RouteFeature + Security + Plan gate
→
Approve planhuman scope decision
Edit serviceguard → write → build → audit
→
Run service testsCSV semantics proven
→
Edit endpointguard → write → build → audit
→
Run auth testsadmin policy proven
Self-reviewconventions + drift + evidence
→
Boy Scoutbounded cleanup candidates
→
Human reviewcorrectness + understanding
→
CI + mergeindependent gates + owner decision
The framework's job: make the expected process repeatable, surface relevant knowledge, prevent defined shortcuts, and demand evidence. The team's job: provide truth, make decisions, understand the code, and own the outcome.

This is the summary to leave on screen for questions. Walk backward to any event or artifact the audience wants to inspect.

AEvent and file reference

What fires when

TriggerRegistered mechanismPrimary effectStrength / caveat
Assistant startssession-start.*Inject bounded Git, adoption, debt, security, wiki, and hazard contextContext; client/version dependent outside Claude
User submits promptroute-prompt.*Classify intent; inject workflow, security, and plan railsInstruction salience; explicit command bypasses classifier
Editor Write/Edit proposedguard.*Deny defined secrets, suppressions, and test defeatsHard block on supported surfaces; shell writes are invisible
Editor Write/Edit succeedspost-write.*Run fast stack feedback and surface failureHook execution/model visibility differs by client
Editor Write/Edit succeedsaudit-trail.*Append timestamp, branch, and relative file pathSide-effect where hook runs; not correctness evidence
Claude turn stops / Copilot next promptboy-scout-check.*Report bounded cleanup candidates with set-based deduplicationSoft nudge; silence may mean already reported
Workflow subtask completesCommand contract + repository scriptsRun build, relevant tests, lint/static checksExplicit action with deterministic command result
Push / PRConfigured CIIndependently rerun mandatory gatesOnly guaranteed if platform owner wired it as blocking
First diagnostic: run pwsh scripts/framework-doctor.ps1 or bash scripts/framework-doctor.sh on each machine before relying on client-specific hook behaviour.

This appendix is the operational lookup. The shipped enforcement-surfaces document carries the detailed, version-sensitive truth table.
